topics.title: Coral Reefs
topics.description: Coral reefs are underwater ecosystems built by tiny animals called coral polyps. They protect coastlines and provide shelter for thousands of species.
topics.quickFact: A healthy reef can support one quarter of all marine species.
topics.imageAlt: Illustration of fish and coral reef structures.

audio.text: Coral reefs are built by small coral animals and help protect many sea creatures.

topics.title: Blue Whales
topics.description: Blue whales are the largest animals on Earth. They communicate with deep sounds that travel long distances through the ocean.
topics.quickFact: A blue whale heart can weigh as much as a small car.
topics.imageAlt: Illustration of a blue whale swimming in open water.

audio.text: Blue whales are giant mammals and use low-frequency calls to communicate.

topics.title: Sea Turtles
topics.description: Sea turtles migrate across entire oceans and return to beaches to lay eggs. Their survival depends on clean shores and protected nesting areas.
topics.quickFact: Some sea turtles travel more than 1,000 miles between feeding and nesting grounds.
topics.imageAlt: Illustration of a sea turtle near waves.

audio.text: Sea turtles migrate long distances and return to beaches where they were born.

topics.title: Jellyfish
topics.description: Jellyfish drift with currents and use tentacles to capture food. Some species glow in the dark using bioluminescence.
topics.quickFact: Jellyfish have existed for over 500 million years.
topics.imageAlt: Illustration of jellyfish floating in deep water.

audio.text: Jellyfish move with ocean currents and some species can produce light.

topics.title: Mangrove Forests
topics.description: Mangrove trees grow where land and sea meet. Their roots create nurseries for young fish and protect coastlines from storms.
topics.quickFact: Mangroves store large amounts of carbon and help fight climate change.
topics.imageAlt: Illustration of mangrove roots and fish.

audio.text: Mangrove roots provide homes for young fish and protect coastlines from erosion.
